[2018-05-28 21:20] megalex: What are the blue and red squares that appear before a game for?
[2018-05-28 21:22] namington: those are bonus unit cards
[2018-05-28 21:22] namington: they're what's used to determine whether the game is b+8, 9, 10, or 11
[2018-05-28 21:23] namington: by default, its 8, but players can contribute bonus unit cards relative to their ranked ticket - for each blue one drawn, +1 unit.
[2018-05-28 21:24] megalex: In which game mode do they show up?
[2018-05-28 21:24] megalex: Oh ok cool thanks. I never tried ranked
[2018-05-28 21:25] swapgo: Does it not show in B+5?
[2018-05-28 21:26] namington: base+5 is always b+5
[2018-05-28 21:26] namington: which can make it a bit overwhelming to go from b+5 straight to b+10 or b+11
[2018-05-28 21:28] swapgo: So its only in casual where you can do B+6/7
[2018-05-28 21:28] scythius: When exactly do you get Omni Cores?
[2018-05-28 21:29] swapgo: You can get them from tickets (I think), one of the casual wins thresholds is an Omni Core
[2018-05-28 21:29] swapgo: Also I've been told that once you exceed certain amount of collection owned
[2018-05-28 21:29] swapgo: You're given the option to trade in a large amount of regular cores for Omni Cores
[2018-05-28 21:30] scythius: regular cores only get me emotes 80% of the time or more
[2018-05-28 21:30] swapgo: Yeah they're mostly for emotes, very rarely for Country Drones
[2018-05-28 21:30] swapgo: Sometimes you hit a jackpot and can get something meaty
[2018-05-28 21:31] swapgo: There's actually an achievement for rolling four reds without spending shards
[2018-05-28 21:31] scythius: good to know I'm not the only one!
[2018-05-28 21:32] megalex: When you open the first core, is there more chances to get better stuff? It's a mini tutorial that gives free crystals too
[2018-05-28 21:33] amalloy: yes, the first one is rigged
[2018-05-28 21:33] scythius: I think the first one is rigged to be good
[2018-05-28 21:33] swapgo: What did you get on the first one
[2018-05-28 21:33] swapgo: Wondering if it's the same because I always stumble into the same Doomed Mechs lol
[2018-05-28 21:34] jiecut: They give you 2 flips, but if its bad I've seen them give another flip. (First armory)
[2018-05-28 21:34] awaclus: I'm pretty sure the first armory has been rigged to always give the exact same flips
[2018-05-28 21:34] megalex: I got 2 flips and got Canada drone, rectang Mahar rect. And tiger shadow Fang
[2018-05-28 21:34] swapgo: Yeah its always the same apparently
[2018-05-28 21:35] scythius: I used to spend all shards on regular cores until I discovered black cards.... :/
[2018-05-28 21:35] awaclus: I feel better about spending shards on cores than black cards really
[2018-05-28 21:35] megalex: What are black cards
[2018-05-28 21:35] megalex: I have 2 power core and 0 omni
[2018-05-28 21:35] awaclus: every time you open cores, you make progress towards getting a black lab card
[2018-05-28 21:35] scythius: save your shards for black lab cards
[2018-05-28 21:36] scythius: I find spending shards on them much more rewarding
[2018-05-28 21:36] megalex: So don't spend shards on regular cores for more flips?
[2018-05-28 21:36] awaclus: which allows you to enter the black lab, where you'll get better rewards than regular cores
[2018-05-28 21:36] swapgo: If there's something you reaaaally want and you flipped 3 Reds/Blues it might be worth but usually its better to spend on Omni/Black Cards
[2018-05-28 21:37] awaclus: I wouldn't say don't spend shards on regular cores, just don't do it unless you're close to getting something you want
[2018-05-28 21:37] megalex: How much cash have you guys spent on shards?
[2018-05-28 21:37] awaclus: I bought the Engineer tier for $25 which included 2k shards, haven't spent any other money
[2018-05-28 21:37] swapgo: Maybe when my Country flag is in the game but that won't happen in a while
[2018-05-28 21:37] scythius: yeah, bought the engineer tier only here
[2018-05-28 21:38] swapgo: I plan on picking up a tier eventually but I don't have that much money to work with
[2018-05-28 21:38] awaclus: I'm going to upgrade to the relevant tiers when the puzzle packs come out

[2018-05-29 08:02] p0lari (Polari): re: rating chats on #ask_a_dev, it sounds like people are way overthinking this
[2018-05-29 08:02] p0lari (Polari): a specific rating isn't even supposed to imply a specific level of skill, it's just an arbitrary number that indicates your skill relative to the rest of the current player pool
[2018-05-29 08:03] p0lari (Polari): if you just keep playing your rating will naturally adjust to whatever is "correct" at any given time
[2018-05-29 08:03] p0lari (Polari): if somebody takes a long break they might be off, but they aren't going to be *that* far off, and as they play more theirs is going to get corrected too
[2018-05-29 08:05] p0lari (Polari): it's not like your rating is perfectly accurate and static anyways, you can have swings of like 200 points by just playing sloppier on some days and tighter on others
[2018-05-29 08:06] p0lari (Polari): the worst that can happen if someone's rating is "wrong" is they'll get poor matches for a few games before the rating adjusts again
[2018-05-29 08:06] p0lari (Polari): who cares about that really

[2018-05-29 13:54] medar: That would just give everyone worse matches
[2018-05-29 13:55] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yes. It has been stated and a reset literally makes no sense.
[2018-05-29 13:55] interjection. (Interjection): Can you explain to me how ELO inflation works?
[2018-05-29 13:55] interjection. (Interjection): I was just pondering solutions
[2018-05-29 13:55] interjection. (Interjection): Or perhaps it's not important
[2018-05-29 13:56] p0lari (Polari): so imagine a whole bunch of players play games under an Elo (not ELO) system and end up at whatever ratings
[2018-05-29 13:56] p0lari (Polari): let's say you're 1700 now
[2018-05-29 13:56] p0lari (Polari): then you double the number of players in pool and all the new players suck
[2018-05-29 13:57] p0lari (Polari): they start at 1500 but fall down by losing to the existing players
[2018-05-29 13:57] p0lari (Polari): the difference between 1500 and where they end up gets distributed among the old players's ratings
[2018-05-29 13:57] interjection. (Interjection): Then their points travel upwards
[2018-05-29 13:57] p0lari (Polari): so maybe you're 1750 now
[2018-05-29 13:57] p0lari (Polari): without having improved
[2018-05-29 13:57] interjection. (Interjection): it just trickles through the leaderboard
[2018-05-29 13:57] p0lari (Polari): now an old player who's also just as good as before comes back after a break
[2018-05-29 13:57] interjection. (Interjection): Do players start at 1500?
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): he has a rating of 1600 but is actually a 1650 player in the new player pool
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): so his rating is "wrong"
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): because of inflation
[2018-05-29 13:58] interjection. (Interjection): It's self correcting though
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): I don't know/remember what number players start at in prismata
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): it doesn't change the dynamics though
[2018-05-29 13:58] p0lari (Polari): and yes it's self correcting
[2018-05-29 13:59] p0lari (Polari): and it doesn't really produce bigger swings than your normal rating swings
[2018-05-29 13:59] 307th: i would guess that it's more that there had been elo deflation for a few years while prismata was in closed alpha
[2018-05-29 13:59] 307th: since people were getting better, but average rating was staying the same
[2018-05-29 13:59] interjection. (Interjection): How does the tier system work? Origanally I would have thought that you can only be matched with people in the same tier. I suppose that's not the case because of the playerbase size?
[2018-05-29 13:59] 307th: so introducing a bunch of new players is correcting for that
[2018-05-29 14:00] p0lari (Polari): yeah that's the same thing with slightly different bias, you play, settle on a rating, others keep improving without the player pool changing, now you're overrated
[2018-05-29 14:00] wilson0862 (Wilson): My real question is why is "ELO" so bothersome? lmao
[2018-05-29 14:00] p0lari (Polari): though that gets compensated by you having vacation penalty and probably dropping closer to your real rank in the process of dropping it
[2018-05-29 14:00] p0lari (Polari): it just bothers me that people go out of their way to capitalise something wrong
[2018-05-29 14:01] p0lari (Polari): like if you type everything lowercase that's just lazy
[2018-05-29 14:01] p0lari (Polari): but why go through extra effort to make something wrong
[2018-05-29 14:01] wilson0862 (Wilson): So if it was actually ELO, Elo would not be annoying?
[2018-05-29 14:02] p0lari (Polari): it would be annoying for the same reasons
[2018-05-29 14:02] p0lari (Polari): I mean if you type "elo" amidst otherwise all lowercase text whatever
[2018-05-29 14:02] p0lari (Polari): but if you're going to spend effort capitalising stuff then come on do it correctly at least
[2018-05-29 14:02] wilson0862 (Wilson): Oh, so the baseline is no caps. Any caps should be correct or 😠.
[2018-05-29 14:03] interjection. (Interjection): Aren't you spending effort explaining all this?
[2018-05-29 14:03] wilson0862 (Wilson): Quite the non sequitur friend.
[2018-05-29 14:04] wilson0862 (Wilson): But anyway, tier is just for display. Even if you're not master you have a hidden rating.
[2018-05-29 14:04] thrarxx (Thrar): @Polari do you know what the purpose is of having both a tier system and an Elo rating?
how do the tiers even work? there's a tooltip that says it can't go down until tier 3, but i'm tier 5 now and still never see it go down
is the tier more like levels that you just gain until you hit 10 one day?
[2018-05-29 14:05] interjection. (Interjection): But then in theory you could practice tons of games against good players in vs mode before going on ladder. Then when you finally do you would climb really quickly and only be matched vs good players and have a hard time actually making it to master tier
[2018-05-29 14:05] thrarxx (Thrar): at first i thought it was kind of like HS's ranks and legend rating, but it doesn't seem to work that way
[2018-05-29 14:05] p0lari (Polari): the tier system is for new player experience, it's really common among different games
[2018-05-29 14:05] p0lari (Polari): in pure Elo you start overrated, get matched against too strong opponents, and it's just depressing seeing your number constantly falling
[2018-05-29 14:06] p0lari (Polari): so you hide it behind some other system where you only make progress
[2018-05-29 14:06] p0lari (Polari): and either never show the real rating or only show it much later
[2018-05-29 14:06] interjection. (Interjection): Perhaps it could be made so that new players hidden rating adjusts really quickly without effecting their opponents as much

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-05-29) ---
[2018-05-29 14:07] thrarxx (Thrar): doesn't make much sense to me, why are lower-level players not allowed to see what skill they are?
[2018-05-29 14:07] interjection. (Interjection): I found it quite exciting when I finally got to see my rating when I made it to Master Tier
[2018-05-29 14:07] wilson0862 (Wilson): Literally just explained it.
[2018-05-29 14:07] interjection. (Interjection): I think it's good like this
[2018-05-29 14:07] thrarxx (Thrar): i agree you shouldn't make it start at a too high level, but a ranking system like in HS where you get paired with similar people and can gain or lose makes sense to me
[2018-05-29 14:08] thrarxx (Thrar): seems like an excuse to cover up a badly implemented rating system where you start too high
[2018-05-29 14:08] interjection. (Interjection): before you make it to master tier, I feel like you should be paired with people based on what tier their in
[2018-05-29 14:08] p0lari (Polari): sorry but do you understand how elo works?
[2018-05-29 14:08] interjection. (Interjection): rather than theie elo (not ELO)
[2018-05-29 14:08] p0lari (Polari): everyone starts at an average rating by definition
[2018-05-29 14:08] p0lari (Polari): so everyone who isn't average or better in their first game starts overrated
[2018-05-29 14:09] thrarxx (Thrar): or for @Interjection think about AoE3's ranks, they're more like HS in that way, you start all the way at the bottom and work your way up if you win more than you lose, but you always have some visible measure of your skill
[2018-05-29 14:09] interjection. (Interjection): In AOE3 we use ELO ratings at the top level. But I apreciate you putting it in terms I'm familiar with haha
[2018-05-29 14:10] thrarxx (Thrar): at the top level Elo is fine, what i don't get is why are bad players not allowed to see a measure of their skill?
[2018-05-29 14:10] interjection. (Interjection): Polari said it would make new players feel bad seeing their rank fall as they begin
[2018-05-29 14:10] thrarxx (Thrar): there are systems out there that do it well, AoE3 or HS are examples of such, but prismata chooses to just cover up the rating behind what's basically just a player level
[2018-05-29 14:11] p0lari (Polari): so I googled aoe3's rank system and looks like it's also elo-based, starts you overrated as I said, and displays different numbers
[2018-05-29 14:11] p0lari (Polari): <http://www.agecommunity.com/faq_mp.aspx#moreratings>
[2018-05-29 14:11] awaclus: >So if it was actually ELO, Elo would not be annoying?
well, not as annoying, because if it was actually an abbreviation and people were typing it as though it was a regular proper noun, that's kind of fine because pronouncable abbreviations can become essentially like regular proper nouns
[2018-05-29 14:11] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): I think it's probably for the best Prismata doesn't use Glicko
[2018-05-29 14:11] thrarxx (Thrar): i don't follow the argument that using Elo ratings is the only way to make ratings work when there are good examples that show otherwise
[2018-05-29 14:11] awaclus: but when you're writing a person's surname as though it was an abbreviation, that just doesn't work
[2018-05-29 14:12] p0lari (Polari): that's one way to do it, have real rating and displayed rating separately, with the displayed rating starting low and converging to the real rating
[2018-05-29 14:12] p0lari (Polari): afaik that's how starcraft does it
[2018-05-29 14:12] thrarxx (Thrar): i'm referring to AoE's power rating (PR) which starts you at 1 and you gain levels as you play, but there's a floor at 1 so you can't drop below
[2018-05-29 14:12] p0lari (Polari): yes, and the power rating is explained in the link I posted above
[2018-05-29 14:12] thrarxx (Thrar): but i think the HS example is actually a more user-friendly implementation of a similar system
[2018-05-29 14:12] interjection. (Interjection): You can drop below but it's not visable
[2018-05-29 14:13] p0lari (Polari): and it's basically elo minus a term representing the uncertainty of your rating
[2018-05-29 14:13] thrarxx (Thrar): start at a floor where you can't drop (in HS that's levels 25-20) and above this you have a zero-sum system
[2018-05-29 14:13] p0lari (Polari): so you start out with high uncertainty which drops your power rating to 1 or whatever, and as you play more you converge towards your real rating
[2018-05-29 14:13] p0lari (Polari): which is always there in the background, invisible to you
[2018-05-29 14:14] thrarxx (Thrar): the point is it doesn't drop to 1, it starts at 1 and stays there if you're truly horrible
[2018-05-29 14:14] interjection. (Interjection): What are your thoughts on being matched by what tier your in (so tier 5 would play ideally tier 5 players but sometimes tier 4 & 6)  rather than being matched by hidden MMR?
[2018-05-29 14:14] interjection. (Interjection): I feel like doing it the former way would promote a better sense of progression for new players
[2018-05-29 14:14] interjection. (Interjection): but with a small player base isn't ideal
[2018-05-29 14:15] thrarxx (Thrar): like how in HS if you beat someone below rank 20 you gain a star but they don't lose one - this slightly increases inflation by feeding points into the system, but it solves the problem of frustrating people with their ever-dropping ratings
[2018-05-29 14:15] thrarxx (Thrar): in prismata it seems to me you have 2 experience-based levels, your general level and your ranked tier, and that seems redundant to me
[2018-05-29 14:15] interjection. (Interjection): That explains why getting legend is easier at the end of the month haha
[2018-05-29 14:15] interjection. (Interjection): also bonus win streak stars
[2018-05-29 14:17] thrarxx (Thrar): i was advocating matchmaking by tier just a few days ago, but that was before i knew it's really just another experience level
knowing this i don't think tier is suitable for matchmaking as it is
[2018-05-29 14:17] thrarxx (Thrar): what if you said you get up to experience level 40 to unlock all blueprints, and then there are 10 more  levels that you can only unlock by playing ranked
[2018-05-29 14:17] p0lari (Polari): the problem with matching new players in prismata is how rapidly people improve
[2018-05-29 14:17] thrarxx (Thrar): that would at least make it somewhat consistent
[2018-05-29 14:18] interjection. (Interjection): Matchmaking by tier, it would give a better sense of the difficulty of your opponent increasing
[2018-05-29 14:18] p0lari (Polari): at any given point there just aren't many fresh tier 1 players to match you against
[2018-05-29 14:18] p0lari (Polari): afaik the game does prefer doing that when possible
[2018-05-29 14:18] interjection. (Interjection): as you go up tiers you would notice your opponent gets better than the current system
[2018-05-29 14:18] thrarxx (Thrar): i would love matchmaking by tier if the tier represented skill in any way, but it seems it only represents amount of matches won
[2018-05-29 14:18] interjection. (Interjection): Currently you could just be really good in tier 4 and then only be matched against other really good players

messages.content: or for @Interjection think about AoE3's ranks, they're more like HS in that way, you start all the way at the bottom and work your way up if you win more than you lose, but you always have some visible measure of your skill

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: In AOE3 we use ELO ratings at the top level. But I apreciate you putting it in terms I'm familiar with haha

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: at the top level Elo is fine, what i don't get is why are bad players not allowed to see a measure of their skill?

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: Polari said it would make new players feel bad seeing their rank fall as they begin

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: there are systems out there that do it well, AoE3 or HS are examples of such, but prismata chooses to just cover up the rating behind what's basically just a player level

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: so I googled aoe3's rank system and looks like it's also elo-based, starts you overrated as I said, and displays different numbers

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: <http://www.agecommunity.com/faq_mp.aspx#moreratings>

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content:
>So if it was actually ELO, Elo would not be annoying?
well, not as annoying, because if it was actually an abbreviation and people were typing it as though it was a regular proper noun, that's kind of fine because pronouncable abbreviations can become essentially like regular proper nouns

messages.author: valgrim_the_demon
messages.content: I think it's probably for the best Prismata doesn't use Glicko

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: i don't follow the argument that using Elo ratings is the only way to make ratings work when there are good examples that show otherwise

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: but when you're writing a person's surname as though it was an abbreviation, that just doesn't work

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: that's one way to do it, have real rating and displayed rating separately, with the displayed rating starting low and converging to the real rating

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: afaik that's how starcraft does it

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: i'm referring to AoE's power rating (PR) which starts you at 1 and you gain levels as you play, but there's a floor at 1 so you can't drop below

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: yes, and the power rating is explained in the link I posted above

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: but i think the HS example is actually a more user-friendly implementation of a similar system

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: You can drop below but it's not visable

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: and it's basically elo minus a term representing the uncertainty of your rating

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: start at a floor where you can't drop (in HS that's levels 25-20) and above this you have a zero-sum system

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: so you start out with high uncertainty which drops your power rating to 1 or whatever, and as you play more you converge towards your real rating

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: which is always there in the background, invisible to you

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: the point is it doesn't drop to 1, it starts at 1 and stays there if you're truly horrible

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: What are your thoughts on being matched by what tier your in (so tier 5 would play ideally tier 5 players but sometimes tier 4 & 6)  rather than being matched by hidden MMR?

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: I feel like doing it the former way would promote a better sense of progression for new players

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: but with a small player base isn't ideal

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: like how in HS if you beat someone below rank 20 you gain a star but they don't lose one - this slightly increases inflation by feeding points into the system, but it solves the problem of frustrating people with their ever-dropping ratings

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: in prismata it seems to me you have 2 experience-based levels, your general level and your ranked tier, and that seems redundant to me

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: That explains why getting legend is easier at the end of the month haha

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: also bonus win streak stars

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content:
i was advocating matchmaking by tier just a few days ago, but that was before i knew it's really just another experience level
knowing this i don't think tier is suitable for matchmaking as it is

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: what if you said you get up to experience level 40 to unlock all blueprints, and then there are 10 more  levels that you can only unlock by playing ranked

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: the problem with matching new players in prismata is how rapidly people improve

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: that would at least make it somewhat consistent

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: Matchmaking by tier, it would give a better sense of the difficulty of your opponent increasing

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: at any given point there just aren't many fresh tier 1 players to match you against

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: afaik the game does prefer doing that when possible

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: as you go up tiers you would notice your opponent gets better than the current system

messages.author: thrarxx
messages.content: i would love matchmaking by tier if the tier represented skill in any way, but it seems it only represents amount of matches won

messages.author: interjection.
messages.content: Currently you could just be really good in tier 4 and then only be matched against other really good players
